blocks.value: Common/SSH's 'command' field cannot run a multi-word command at all in this Concert install - confirmed live: a bare single word ('whoami') runs fine, but anything with a space fails with bash reporting the ENTIRE string as one not-found command (two different quote-escaping workarounds were tried and both failed identically live, ruling out simple re-quoting). Worked around entirely: the real script is written to the target via Common/SFTP/SFTP Put File (mode 0755, no command execution involved), then invoked via Common/SSH using ONLY its bare path as 'command' (a single word, matching the one case confirmed to work), and its output is retrieved via Common/SFTP/SFTP Get File instead of `cat` over SSH stdout - no multi-word SSH command needed anywhere in this workflow.

Concert only generates a native OS-type auto-remediation action when the scanned host's operating system name matches Concert's expected SBOM OS identifiers (see 'Configuring auto-remediation workflows' in the official docs - 'amazon linux' for Amazon Linux). Confirmed the vm_scan CSV/API metadata has no field for this at all (only optional scanner_name is documented for vm_scan). The OS identifier has to come from a ConcertDef deploy SBOM registering the host as a 'vm'-type runtime with properties.os/properties.version - so BuildDeploySbom/UploadDeploySbom register that BEFORE the vm_scan upload attaches CVE data to it.

blocks.name: BuildDeploySbom

blocks.action: system/IBM/Concert v2/Import Data/Upload Files to Concert
blocks.name: UploadDeploySbom

inputs.name: contents
inputs.value:
"#!/bin/bash
command -v trivy >/dev/null 2>&1 || (curl -sfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy/main/contrib/install.sh | sudo sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin v0.72.0)
sudo trivy rootfs --scanners vuln --format json --quiet --output /tmp/trivy-scan.json /
"

blocks.name: BuildVmScanCsv

blocks.action: system/IBM/Concert v2/Import Data/Upload Files to Concert
blocks.name: IngestScan

meta.description: Common/SSH credential stored in Concert for the target host.
Stored Concert API credential (protocol/host/api key/instance id).
IP address or hostname of the scanned host - populates the Host IPAddress/Host Name columns so Concert attributes these CVEs to this specific host, and the deploy SBOM's runtime network/hostname.
Registers/matches the application this host's deploy SBOM is associated with.
Environment name the deploy SBOM registers this runtime under (e.g. production, staging).
Must match one of Concert's expected SBOM OS identifiers exactly (e.g. "amazon linux", "redhat", "ubuntu", "sles", "oracle linux", "windows") for OS-type auto-remediation actions to generate.

Scans a remote Linux host over SSH for OS-level vulnerabilities using Trivy: registers the host as a 'vm'-type runtime with its OS identifier via a ConcertDef deploy SBOM (required for Concert to generate OS-type auto-remediation actions), converts the Trivy result into Concert's native vm_scan CSV format, and ingests both via the official Import Data/Upload Files to Concert block. Uses SFTP Put/Get File to sidestep a live-confirmed bug where Common/SSH's command field cannot run any multi-word command.
